title: Pax Autocratica
anchor_summary: A sci-fi hybrid that mixes colony/base management and policy-driven growth with direct first-person squad combat, plus roguelite run upgrades that feed back into long-term regime expansion.

similar_games.title: Executive Assault 2
similar_games.why_similar: Blends RTS/base building with directly controlled first-person combat in a sci-fi war setting.

similar_games.title: Silica
similar_games.why_similar: Another FPS-and-RTS hybrid where you fight on the ground while also commanding larger battlefield forces.

similar_games.title: The Riftbreaker
similar_games.why_similar: Pairs base expansion, tech progression, and resource logistics with active real-time combat in a hostile sci-fi world.

similar_games.title: Earth From Another Sun
similar_games.why_similar: Open-world sci-fi shooter focused on leading troops, growing power, and mixing personal combat with broader conquest.

similar_games.title: Nuclear Dawn
similar_games.why_similar: A recognizable example of FPS play fused with RTS-style command and teamwide strategic control.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Strong Steam store text describes a hybrid centered on building and governing a colony/state apparatus, expanding facilities, researching technologies, automating production and logistics, managing population conditions, and then personally leading first-person roguelite combat expeditions. That supports colony_sim as the best-fit primary identity, with military_fps and RTS-like command elements as secondary influences. Evidence is somewhat noisy because the Metacritic description appears mismatched to another game, but the Steam detailed and short descriptions are concrete and substantial enough on their own to support acceptance.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Blends RTS/base building with directly controlled first-person combat in a sci-fi war setting.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently present Rotwood as a 1-4 player hack-and-slash roguelike/dungeon crawler built around repeat runs, co-op combat, boss fights, weapon mastery, gear upgrading, and bringing spoils back to craft equipment and improve a safe haven. The text explicitly says 'every run offers different powers and abilities,' calls it a 'rogue-like dungeon crawler,' emphasizes combat mastery over randomness, and highlights increasingly difficult encounters and powerful corrupted bosses. Metacritic also labels it a '2D Beat-'Em-Up,' which supports the side-scrolling/beat-em-up secondary read, but the stronger identity is cooperative action roguelite due to run-based structure, repeatable progression, and meta/base advancement.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Co-op action roguelite with fast melee combat, repeat runs, build experimentation, and boss-driven progression.
Co-op-capable dungeon-running action RPG with skill-based combat, persistent progression, and runs centered on surviving escalating encounters.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored DLC descriptions consistently describe a combat-centric action game built around three new campaign chapters, unique levels, new weapons, bosses, fiend enemies, deadly trials, and an endurance-style challenge mode with successive combat encounters. The weapon copy emphasizes ranged attack, crowd control, rapid strikes, closing distance, and testing skilled 'master ninja' play, which strongly supports a fast, mastery-heavy character-action identity. The campaign/chapter structure and boss/trial language support linear level-based progression rather than open-world or RPG-first play. Horror imagery is present in the setting and story framing, but the gameplay evidence is much more strongly centered on stylish high-intensity combat than on horror as the primary play identity.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: This DLC directly inherits the base game's core mission-based campaign structure, weapon-driven combo combat, enemy gauntlets, and challenge-focused progression.

title: Apopia: Sugar Coated Tale
anchor_summary: A single-player, story-driven indie adventure built around hand-drawn exploration, character-heavy writing, puzzle solving, and frequent gameplay shifts into minigames/rhythm/light action, with a cute surface that gradually reveals darker emotional mysteries.

similar_games.title: Undertale
similar_games.why_similar: Quirky narrative adventure with memorable NPCs, dark-under-the-surface storytelling, and encounters resolved through distinctive minigame-style sequences rather than straightforward combat.

similar_games.title: Beacon Pines
similar_games.why_similar: Cute storybook presentation paired with mystery, branching narrative beats, and an increasingly unsettling tone beneath a charming exterior.

similar_games.title: Wandersong
similar_games.why_similar: Character-driven indie adventure mixing exploration, puzzles, dialogue, and music/rhythm-led interactions in a whimsical world with emotional payoff.

similar_games.title: Night in the Woods
similar_games.why_similar: Narrative-first adventure focused on conversations, personal identity, and slow-burn darkness, with light minigame variety supporting the story.

similar_games.title: Little Misfortune
similar_games.why_similar: A cute-yet-macabre adventure centered on a young protagonist, odd characters, and unsettling story reveals delivered through simple exploration and interaction.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored evidence consistently describes a single-player hand-drawn narrative adventure with puzzles, rhythm segments, minigames, light action, and character-focused mystery. However, the copy does not establish a stable core gameplay loop, perspective, traversal model, or dominant mechanics strongly enough to support any allowed V2 archetype. It is not clearly a visual novel, hidden-object puzzle game, action-platformer, psychological horror title, or cinematic action-adventure based on the available text. The gameplay appears intentionally mixed and provider copy stays broad, so the evidence is insufficient for a confident archetype assignment.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Quirky narrative adventure with memorable NPCs, dark-under-the-surface storytelling, and encounters resolved through distinctive minigame-style sequences rather than straightforward combat.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Classification is strongly supported by the stored store/editorial copy plus a direct Metacritic genre label of '2D Beat-'Em-Up.' The text repeatedly frames the game as a 'brand-new brawling adventure' centered on fighting through Toronto, stringing together stylish combos, using weapons and special attacks, battling enemies and bosses, and supporting up to four-player co-op. It also specifies a 2D beat-'em-up identity, seven fighters with distinct combat styles, and a city of interconnected levels with quests and hidden areas, which fits a campaign-driven beat_em_up with semi-open progression and light action-adventure structure rather than a pure linear stage arcade brawler. Progression is reinforced by badges, stat upgrades, coins, and customizable builds.

similar_games.title: Torment: Tides of Numenera
similar_games.why_similar: Modern Torment-style RPG built around text-heavy encounters, stat checks, consequential choices, and exploration of a strange setting.

similar_games.title: Citizen Sleeper
similar_games.why_similar: Also emphasizes dice-driven checks, investigation, faction pressures, and branching narrative progression over traditional combat-heavy RPG structure.

similar_games.title: Pentiment
similar_games.why_similar: Strong fit for its authored mystery-solving, dialogue-led roleplay, and consequential social investigation, even though it is less of a full CRPG.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently identify Esoteric Ebb as an isometric single-player CRPG with deep branching dialogue, abundant choices, open-ended quests, dice-driven TTRPG-style encounters, investigation of a political conspiracy, and exploration of a fantasy city. The strongest identity signals are authored branching narrative, quest-solving, detective-mystery framing, and classic CRPG structure rather than party tactics or action combat. Combat is explicitly de-emphasized ('violence as a last resort'), so western_narrative_rpg fits better than party_based_crpg or combat-forward RPG archetypes.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored store copy is strong and concrete: the game is repeatedly described as a 'third-person arcade racer' focused on licensed car racing, online races, global time trials, and weekly events. Core play is competing in races, chasing lap times, and progressing through a motorsport legacy structure by earning resources and investing in team growth, but the identity remains vehicular racing first. The 'easy to learn yet endlessly rewarding' physics, stylized recreations of licensed cars/tracks, and explicit arcade-racer phrasing support arcade_racer over realistic_racer, while the management layer appears secondary rather than transforming it into a sim/tycoon game.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Arcade-style motorsport racing paired with team upgrades, season progression, and managing resources between events.

The copy also states players 'finish off the numerous enemies with their very own challenges and tactics' and confront the villain Grabbo, which is enough to establish meaningful combat alongside dominant platform traversal. This supports action_platformer over other platformer families: it is clearly not a metroidvania, not a 3D collectathon, and the emphasis is broader arcade stage-clearing rather than precision-only challenge branding.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Store and aggregator descriptions consistently describe a single-player, third-person cozy gardening game set in historical France. The core loop is restoring and tending a garden in a free-form natural space, growing and identifying plants through simulated ecosystem factors like soil, moisture, and plant proximity, while progressing gentle quests, uncovering hidden objects, helping NPCs, and pursuing romance. The game is explicitly peaceful and non-combat, with linear story advancement layered onto an at-your-own-pace life/gardening routine. That aligns best with farming_sim, with life_sim as a secondary influence due to social/romance and community-help elements.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Free-form cozy gardening focused on planting, tending, and restoring a space at your own pace rather than running a traditional farm loop.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored evidence consistently describes Soulshard as a 2D side-scrolling platformer built around 30 discrete levels, trap avoidance, and using the environment to progress, with single-player and local co-op play. The copy does not establish meaningful combat, RPG systems, exploration structure, or action-platformer fighting, which points away from action_platformer and toward a non-combat precision/platform-navigation identity. Minimalist pixel-art presentation and a dark limbo-like setting are also directly supported by the store text.

The gameplay identity is therefore best matched as visual_novel.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently portray Aquamarine as a single-player, turn-based sci-fi survival/exploration adventure focused on foraging, cultivation, salvage, loadout upgrades, navigational challenges, and environmental puzzle solving in an alien ocean. The strongest supported signals are cursor-driven/point-and-click interaction, authored linear mystery framing, non-combat play, and environmental puzzles. However, none of the allowed Similar Games Taxonomy V2 archetypes cleanly fits this combination. It is not well-supported as hidden_object_puzzle because the evidence emphasizes survival, navigation, and environmental problem solving rather than hidden-object scenes. It is not well-supported as management_tycoon because there is no strong business/optimization/tycoon loop. It also does not cleanly map to exploration_survival_adventure because the available archetype implies a more standard real-time survival-adventure identity than the game's turn-based, comic-book, puzzle-forward structure. Because the evidence is solid about what the game is, but does not strongly support any allowed archetype, rejection is the safest taxonomy outcome.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Closest match for turn-based sci-fi survival built around resource decisions, hazardous exploration, and uncovering a larger mystery rather than combat.
Shares expedition-based runs, survival pressure, resource planning, and event-driven exploration with a roguelike structure.
A journey-focused survival game centered on managing supplies, party condition, and route hazards across an authored campaign.
Exploration and survival across a hostile world map with strong narrative discovery, resource upkeep, and gradual uncovering of lore.
Strong alien-ocean survival and discovery comp: scavenging, crafting upgrades, studying wildlife, and solving navigation problems to get home.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored official descriptions repeatedly frame the game as 'survival horror' and add concrete loop details: combat, investigations, puzzles, and resource management. The corpus also explicitly says players can freely switch between first- and third-person views, supporting a modern single-player campaign survival-horror structure rather than a purely cinematic or purely psychological horror title. Story setup emphasizes investigating mysterious deaths in Raccoon City while escaping lethal threats, which supports detective_mystery plus survival_escape within an authored linear campaign.

title: Laysara: Summit Kingdom
anchor_summary: A single-player, economy-first city builder focused on settlement growth, production chains, terrain-constrained logistics, and surviving environmental pressures rather than combat.

similar_games.title: Frostpunk
similar_games.why_similar: Harsh-environment city building centered on survival, resource balancing, infrastructure planning, and adapting your settlement to deadly weather threats.

similar_games.title: Against the Storm
similar_games.why_similar: Multi-settlement management with interconnected progression, supply logistics, and constant optimization around hostile environmental conditions.

similar_games.title: Banished
similar_games.why_similar: A combat-free settlement survival builder where production chains, population needs, and careful resource planning determine whether a remote town survives.

similar_games.title: Anno 1800
similar_games.why_similar: Complex economy and transport-network optimization built around chaining production, satisfying tiered citizen needs, and linking settlements through trade.

similar_games.title: Timberborn
similar_games.why_similar: Systemic colony/city building driven by terrain-aware infrastructure, resource routing, and preparing for recurring natural hazards in a sandbox loop.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently and explicitly identify Laysara: Summit Kingdom as a 'challenging city builder' and a 'pure city building experience' focused on economy, resource management, production chains, settlement expansion, transport/logistics optimization, hazard mitigation, and rebuilding across multiple mountain towns. The core loop is establishing and expanding settlements, satisfying population needs, building trade and transport networks, and solving environmental constraints like avalanches and weather. The text also directly states there is no combat or military aspect. This strongly supports city_builder as the primary archetype, with management_tycoon/colony_sim as adjacent secondary readings due to resource chains, needs management, and multi-settlement administration.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Harsh-environment city building centered on survival, resource balancing, infrastructure planning, and adapting your settlement to deadly weather threats.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently identify the game as a colorful 2D platformer and remake of the 1988 platformer The NewZealand Story. The gameplay loop is described as jumping and running through handcrafted levels, uncovering hidden areas, using weapons, vehicles, and power-ups, and facing challenging enemies and bosses. This supports a level-based side-scrolling action-platformer identity with platform navigation as the core verb and combat as a supporting but meaningful layer. Although the computed near-miss/candidate system surfaced beat_em_up from side-scrolling + melee + bosses, the text strongly centers platforming, responsive movement, precision, hidden secrets, and arcade stage challenge rather than crowd-control brawling, making action_platformer the better fit.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: It is the original game this remake is built on, sharing the same core side-scrolling platforming, enemy layouts, weapons, vehicles, and rescue-driven arcade structure.
Another arcade-rooted 2D action-platformer focused on tough stage clears, projectile combat, hazards, and boss fights.
Classic side-scrolling platformer lineage with bright arcade presentation, level progression, enemies, secrets, and old-school challenge.
A polished 2D platformer with responsive movement, level-based progression, hidden content, and frequent action-platforming set pieces.

title: The Demon Queen's Dire Dilemma
anchor_summary: A short, linear, choice-free yuri visual novel centered on comedic character banter and romance, with a cute anime presentation and light fantasy framing.

similar_games.title: Sweetest Monster
similar_games.why_similar: Another yuri-focused visual novel from ebi-hime with a short, character-driven reading experience and strong emphasis on romantic comedy/dialogue over gameplay systems.

similar_games.title: Kindred Spirits on the Roof
similar_games.why_similar: A well-known yuri visual novel built around reading, relationship development, and lighthearted character interactions rather than branching mechanics or action.

similar_games.title: Highway Blossoms
similar_games.why_similar: A recognizable romance-first yuri VN with a relaxed, story-led structure and a strong focus on dialogue, chemistry, and emotional payoff.

similar_games.title: Nurse Love Addiction
similar_games.why_similar: Another anime-style yuri visual novel where the core loop is reading character scenes and following relationship drama in a mostly traditional VN format.

similar_games.title: The Fairy's Song
similar_games.why_similar: A fantasy-tinged yuri visual novel from the same creator, sharing the same emphasis on accessible reading, character banter, and romance-driven storytelling.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored evidence is strong and direct for a visual novel classification. Multiple store/editorial descriptions explicitly call it a 'visual novel' and describe '60k of text,' '2-4 hours of reading,' and 'a linear narrative with no choices or branches.' The gameplay identity is therefore narrative-reading focused rather than action, strategy, or exploration driven. Supporting facets include single-player mode, cute anime-style presentation, casual genre labeling, and absence of combat mechanics. The premise centers on character interaction between the demon queen and princess, supporting an authored linear interpersonal drama structure within a fantasy setting.

resolved_similar_games.why_similar: Another yuri-focused visual novel from ebi-hime with a short, character-driven reading experience and strong emphasis on romantic comedy/dialogue over gameplay systems.
